Gen AI Agents Playing Mafia Game. This repository contains a simple web app for playing a Mafia game where all the players are AI agents powered by large language models. The frontend is written in vanilla JavaScript and the backend is a small Flask server that orchestrates the game logic.

Project aims to leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) to enhance  customer support interactions by providing intelligent, context-aware responses. It  seeks to seamlessly integrate LLM technology with databases, PDF knowledge bases,  and audio processing agents, creating a comprehensive customer support application.

Explainable X is an AI-powered Python programming assistant desktop application that uses large language models to help users develop Python projects. Through a graphical interface, users can interact with AI agents to generate code, modify files, manage projects, and more.

An AI-driven Search Engine Optimization (SEO) automation suite designed to streamline and enhance various SEO processes. It leverages intelligent agents and Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate tasks such as keyword research, on-page optimization, content generation, and technical SEO.

DeepInsight is an intelligent document processing system that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) and agentic AI to extract structured knowledge from unstructured documents. The system automatically generates ontologies, extracts entities and relationships, and prepares data for loading into graph databases.

This repository contains the code for simulating and analyzing the behavior of Large Language Model (LLM) agents in a Spatial Prisoner's Dilemma (SPD) game. The simulation explores how various factors, such as the number of rounds and access to payoff information, influence the cooperation and decision-making strategies of LLM agents.

JARVIS is an advanced digital agent project based on LLMs (Large Language Models) with a strong focus on adaptive personalization. The goal is to create a system that continuously learns from user input and feedback, improving interaction quality and offering a tailored experience.

This project implements a sophisticated ReAct (Actor-Critic) Agent enhanced with a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline for handling insurance policy queries. The system combines the reasoning capabilities of a Large Language Model (LLM) with the precision of retrieved context from insurance documentation.

Implementation of Language-Driven Play: Large Language Models as Game-Playing Agents in Slay the Spire by Bahar Bateni and Jim Whitehead. We reuse their MiniStS code, but we add more cards and scenarios. We use LLMs as general game playing agents by understanding cards and playing out the game using that knowledge.

A powerful research assistant that leverages Large Language Models to provide technically-focused, well-researched answers with robust citations. Built on a graph-based architecture, the agent drafts answers, performs critical self-reflection, conducts research, and iteratively improves responses based on discovered information.

An interactive Streamlit app showcasing the evolution of Large Language Models (LLMs) — from basic text queries to advanced agentic RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) integrations using Google Gemini. Features include model selection, document-based question answering, GitHub tool integration, and interactive workflow visualizations.

Self-refining agent that converts hardware descriptions (Verilog/VHDL) into formal proofs using Large Language Models and deductive search. Based on MIT's "LLM-Assisted Circuit Proofs" research (May 2025), this tool automates the verification of digital circuits using Isabelle and Coq theorem provers.

An interactive Streamlit application that combines machine learning, data visualization, and large language models (LLMs) to act as a virtual data analyst. This project lets users upload datasets, auto-generate insights, and query their data through a conversational AI agent powered by Google Gemini.

This repository provides an introduction to the basics of the LangChain library, a tool for building applications with Large Language Models. It covers foundational concepts such as prompt templates, LLM chains, memory, and agents, with practical examples. The content focuses on leveraging OpenAI's APIs.

An autonomous multi-agent system built with CrewAI that performs structured financial research and analysis on companies. It uses large language models and integrated search tools to gather company data, analyse performance, summarise recent news, and generate polished financial reports with clear insights and outlooks.

A simple and effective command-line tool to convert emails exported from Gmail (as an .mbox file) into a structured JSON file. This is the first step in building automated workflows and intelligent agents powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) using your email data.

Mini-PestMA is a sophisticated multi-agent system designed to assist in the diagnosis of plant pests and diseases. By leveraging multiple Large Language Models (LLMs) running locally via Ollama, the system provides a robust, evidence-based analysis that goes beyond a single model's opinion.

This project is an AI-powered travel planning assistant that uses a multi-agent workflow to help users plan trips, book flights and hotels, recommend restaurants and more. A Python-powered text-to-SQL project leveraging agentic AI and large language models (LLMs). Users can ask football-related questions in natural English, and the system generates valid SQL queries (with one LLM), executes them on a Postgres database (with another LLM), and returns the results.

The AI LLM Research Crew is an automated research assistant project designed to gather, analyze, and synthesize information about Artificial Intelligence and Large Language Models (LLMs). This project utilizes a crew-based approach, likely employing multiple AI agents working together to conduct comprehensive research on the given topic.

This Streamlit application leverages the power of Groq's Large Language Models (LLMs) to help you identify potential business leads from web page content. You provide URLs and keywords, and the agent scrapes the pages, analyzes the text using an LLM, and presents potential leads based on your criteria.

Project-Alpha is a simulation of the classic board game Risk, where AI agents, powered by various Large Language Models (LLMs), strategize and compete for world domination. The project features a complete game engine handling core Risk rules, integration points for different AI models, communication channels for AI diplomacy

Chat PDF Agent — AI Chatbot for PDFs with Perplexity API and LangChain An interactive Streamlit app to upload multiple PDF files, create a vector store with local sentence-transformer embeddings, and ask detailed questions using the Perplexity API large language model. Enables context-aware conversational search over documents.

An intelligent, conversational CRM system powered by a Large Language Model (LLM) and a Neo4j knowledge graph. This application allows users to interact with their customer data using natural language, upload CSV files to build the knowledge graph, and gain insights through a sophisticated AI agent built with LangChain.

CaritasAI is an intelligent Agentic AI platform that connects Catholic volunteers, parishes, and charities to real-world service opportunities. It uses Large Language Models (LLMs) and geospatial data to match people who want to help with those who need assistance, bridging digital coordination gaps in Catholic social outreach.

HealthFlow AI is a smart, full-stack web application that leverages a large language model (LLM) to act as an intelligent agent for managing medical appointments. The application provides a conversational interface for both patients and doctors, allowing them to handle complex scheduling and reporting tasks using natural language.

RecruitiQ Agency is a next-gen, AI-driven recruitment solution designed to streamline the hiring process using Large Language Models (LLMs), intelligent agents, and real-time resume analysis. Built with Streamlit, Ollama, and modular AI orchestration, it automates candidate matching, ranking, and screening making hiring smarter and faster.

WebPilot AI is a sophisticated, AI-powered browser automation tool. It uses a large language model to interpret natural language instructions and translate them into browser actions, allowing it to perform complex web-based tasks autonomously. The agent's actions are streamed in real-time and recorded for later review.

The goal of this project was to build an AI-powered agent capable of analyzing complex legal documents—specifically the 'Credit Act 2025'—and extracting structured, actionable data. Manual review of such documents is time-consuming and prone to error, so we automated this using a Large Language Model (LLM)."

This Streamlit application is a powerful AI-driven tool that helps users solve math problems and look up general knowledge using the Gemma 2 large language model via Groq. It combines multiple capabilities such as math reasoning, arithmetic calculation, and Wikipedia-based information search—all integrated through LangChain agents and tools.

A sequential Large Language Model (LLM) agent system designed for automated research assistance. It features a four-stage pipeline: Scraping (using Llama 3.2-1b to select links), Summarization, Validation (using Llama 3.3-70b for up-to-date fact-checking), and finally, a QnA/Chat interface for user interaction.

The LLM-Based Automation Agent is a smart API-powered assistant designed to process natural language requests and execute automation tasks efficiently. It integrates an LLM (Large Language Model) to interpret plain-English instructions and perform a wide range of operations-related tasks such as script execution, data processing, and log analysis.

An AI-powered shopping assistant that reads a user’s grocery list and automatically finds and adds matching items to a cart on platforms like Walmart. Built for the UARK AI Workshop, this project explores intelligent agent design, web automation, and real-world integration of large language models (LLMs) and retrieval techniques.

This project is a Streamlit-based web application that allows you to ask questions about the contents of a CSV file using a powerful Large Language Model (LLM) via LangChain. It uses OpenAI's GPT and the create_csv_agent utility from langchain_experimental.agents to enable natural language querying of tabular data.

A smart AI Agent that uses a Large Language Model (LLM) to understand user instructions and take real-world actions — like sending emails, searching Google, or playing YouTube videos — all through natural language.  This is a working prototype built with Python, Playwright, and a local LLM to explore the future of AI-driven automation.
